text:
There once was a small, cozy cottage nestled in a woodsy corner of a lovely, foggy landscape. Our tale begins with Olive, a wise old owl who had watched over the woods for many years. One crisp autumn morning, after a gentle snowfall had turned the forest into a whispering dreamscape, Olive overheard a young squirrel lament.

"I can't seem to find any Acorns this year," the squirrel lamented. "Even my nose is tired of tapping."

Olive blinked in mild curiosity and hooted sympathetically. "How about some Elderberry? They're abundant this season."

With gratitude, the squirrel gathered several of them and hid them among the leaves. The pair became companions, sharing berries and tales under the ancient oak that had witnessed countless changes.

One day, a loud clap echoed across the woods. A branch broke with a disturbing crack, spilling a heap of broken toys into the undergrowth. Startled, the squirrel scuttled away.

Days
